items.q: What are conventions of the Gothic genre?
items.correct: Settings of ruin and decay, supernatural or psychological terror, transgression of boundaries, the sublime, doubled or haunted protagonists, and atmospheric excess.
items.misconception: Gothic just means scary stories with vampires and ghosts.
items.random_wrong: Gothic refers to medieval architecture, not literature.
items.why: Students reduce Gothic to monsters. The genre is defined by its concerns — boundaries, the past intruding on the present, the limits of reason — and only sometimes uses supernatural creatures.

items.q: Why must students distinguish the narrator from the author?
items.correct: The narrator is a constructed voice within the text; the author is the real person who created it. Conflating them misreads irony, unreliable narration and persona.
items.misconception: The narrator's views are always the author's views.
items.random_wrong: The narrator and author are different words for the same thing.
items.why: Students attribute everything a first-person narrator says to the author. This collapses irony — a novel like 'Lolita' cannot work if you take Humbert as Nabokov.

items.q: Why distinguish the speaker of a poem from the poet?
items.correct: The speaker is the voice constructed in the poem; the poet is the writer. Treating them as identical can mislead, especially with dramatic monologues or personae.
items.misconception: The speaker of a poem is always the poet themselves.
items.random_wrong: The speaker is whoever reads the poem out loud.
items.why: Browning's 'My Last Duchess' is not Browning — it is a murderous Duke. Students collapse speaker into poet, losing the persona and the moral complexity that comes with it.

items.q: Which Milton epic does the Creature read and use to frame his own situation?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Paradise Lost. “Paradise Regained” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: On which remote island chain does Victor begin and destroy the female creature?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: The Orkneys. “The Hebrides” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In which year did Freud publish his essay 'The Uncanny' (Das Unheimliche)?
items.why: The real figure is 1919. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
